programmes.show_description: A heavy metal drummer has his life turned upside down when he starts to lose his hearing. Contains very strong language. [2020]
8/8. Tender dominates the headlines, sending shockwaves across the media and the government. Contains strong language, some sexual content and some upsetting scenes.
4/6. Kacey comes round to help bury the jars and asks Michael about the hoarded boxes. Contains some strong language.
Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.
A young boy is shipwrecked on a remote island and must adapt to facing life alone. [2023]
With the new salmon-fishing season about to open on the River Tweed, Sammi Kinghorn and Matt Baker follow the final preparations before the first cast of the year.

programmes.show_name: My Kind of Town: Fort William
programmes.show_description: 6/6. The final episode in this series sees Ian and Major head to Fort William, the biggest town in the Highlands, and the UK's self-styled outdoor capital.
6/29. It's judging day, and only one chef can go through to represent Scotland. Bafta-winning actress Katherine Parkinson helps decide the winner from the two remaining chefs.
More delicious recipes from Richard Bainbridge, Julie Lin, Gaz Oakley and Shauna Froydenlund! Also, Rick Stein and Clive Myrie head out across the UK and abroad.

programmes.show_name: The Life and Death of Colonel Blimp
programmes.show_description: Classic British epic about the life and loves of a reactionary army officer, tracing his career from the Boer War to World War II. [1943]
World War II drama relating the Battle of Britain through the eyes of the operations room of a Kent fighter station during the summer of 1940. [1952]
28/60. Expert Nick Davies is drawn to a bobbin holder that reveals Huddersfield's textile heritage, while Christina Trevanion finds an incredible collection of glass scent bottles.
2/12. Ally is hoping to make his mark at Dingwall Mart, Adam and Lucy are under huge pressure to make the big move earlier than expected, and winter storms cost the Glenn family dearly.
All the talking points and in-depth analysis from Round 4 of the Six Nations.
The roadshow is at Pitzhanger Manor in London, where finds include a quirky 18th-century children's book, a collection of vintage trainers and a Battle of Britain flying helmet.
2/6. The pride's mums flee as intruder males threaten their cubs. Leopard Lediba searches for her lost cub, while cheetah mum Pobe confronts a male leopard to protect her offspring.
Police hunt for a man who has violently sexually attacked a woman in a public phone box. Contains scenes which some viewers may find disturbing.
Saileog Ní Cheannabháin and John Francis Flynn meet in a captivating music session rooted in sean-nós and Ireland's rich traditions of song and story.
Award-winning folk singer Eoghan Ó Ceannabháin meets composer and multi-instrumentalist RÓIS in a vibrant collaboration of contemporary Irish voices.
Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policymakers on the key issues.
War drama following a 19-year-old college drop-out who signs up for service in Vietnam. Contains very strong language, graphic violence and disturbing scenes. [1986]
Barra Best experiences how the climate shapes people's lives and discovers that while nothing can be done to change the weather, people can use it to their advantage.
9/10. The Bowman team embark on a puzzling case when a body is discovered in an Oxford river. Contains some strong language and some upsetting scenes.
Fiona Bruce presents an hour of topical debate from Kettering. On the panel are Stephen Doughty, James Cleverly, Shashank Joshi, George Monbiot and Annabel Denham.
Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.
6/14. Guest Dragon and skincare supremo Susie Ma returns to the Den.
Highlights from the FA Cup fifth round, including League One Mansfield against Premier League leaders Arsenal, and Wrexham, from the Championship, at home to eight-time winners Chelsea.
Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.
The big political issues of the day.
11/30. Game show in which players score points by answering questions correctly while avoiding the Impossible answers.
26/45. Jason Manford hosts the quiz show where players face a question and pick from a board of six rankable answers. Some answers are good, but only one is Unbeatable.
21/25. Susan Calman hosts the quiz show of tactics, knowledge and nerve. Contestants must work as a team, but ultimately there can only be one Boss.
1/5. Derek dons his walking boots and heads for the hills above Aberdyfi.
1/3. Isolated since the time of the dinosaurs, New Zealand's wildlife has been left to its own devices. Its forests are stalked by predators from the Jurassic era.
1/26. Michael Aspel presents his first Antiques Roadshow from the magnificent surroundings of the Victoria and Albert Museum in London.
1/76. The team heads to Malvern, where Adam Partridge finds a set of playing cards with hand-written predictions, and Kate Bateman discovers a mysterious bronze statue of a monk.

programmes.show_name: New: Richard Osman's House of Games
programmes.show_description: 86/100. Former winners Chris McCausland, Claire Richards, Adele Roberts and Olly Smith test their general knowledge skills on this week's Richard Osman's House of Games: Champions.
11/20. Vernon Kay and unreliable guide Paul Gorton challenge Owain Wyn Evans, Greg Rutherford and Abby Cook to separate surprising truths from tall tales in Belfast.
Bringing forensic journalism from all corners of the world.
2/6. Paul explores northern Loch Fyne and discovers the wreckage of the 'diamond bomber'.
25/31. Clive Myrie asks the questions in the first semi-final. The specialist subjects include Fleabag, Michael Faraday, the presidency of Ronald Reagan and British marine invertebrates.
31/37. The quarter-final stage continues. Having won one match already, the teams know that if they can win again here, they'll reach the semi-finals. Amol Rajan asks the questions.
3/3. The hidden link between murder suspect Luigi Mangione and a controversial AI algorithm. Contains some scenes which some viewers may find upsetting.
Pauline Scanlon, Caoimhe Hopkinson and Austin Walkin' Cane bring their talents to this entertaining fusion of musical cultures, stories and legacies from Ireland and the US.
Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.   Followed by Weather.
5/6. Strange things are happening in Marvin Gardens, and they start to attract attention. Contains some strong language.
6/6. Victor and Margaret prepare for a holiday but have to cope with Mrs Warboys's bout of food poisoning, a visit from the electricity man and a snake.
